WHITE, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE, CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY's Motion To Strike Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication is Granted. Defendants' motion for summary judgment, or alternatively summary adjudication, set for January 13, 2012 is off calendar. Defendants did not properly serve their motion on Plaintiffs pursuant to CCP 1011(a). Leaving summary judgment papers outside Plaintiffs' office building ("against your door" or "virtually on the street," as described by Defendants' counsel) does not constitute proper service under CCP 1011(a). Plaintiffs had not agreed to accept service by fax or email. (CCP 1013(e), 1010.6.) Plaintiffs' failure to object to prior service electronically or by fax did not constitute an agreement by Plaintiffs to accept service of summary judgment motion by email and/or fax. In any event, service by email was not timely; the substance of the summary judgment motion did not go through. Defendants' service by mail is untimely.
In addition, the motion for summary judgment that Defendants contend they served by hand on Plaintiffs on October 28, 2011 was substantially different than the motion for summary judgment Defendants filed with the Court on November 7, 2011.
Plaintiff's objections to evidence are waived. Evidence in support of opposition submitted to the court is filed. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918
